title: Leucas zeylanica
taxonomy: Leucas zeylanica is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Leucas, which consists of various species of flowering plants. Leucas zeylanica is a native species found in the tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Leucas zeylanica is further detailed under the order Lamiales and kingdom Plantae. uses: Leucas zeylanica is a plant species traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine. intro: Leucas zeylanica, also known as the "Elephant's Tooth" or "Wild Curry Leaf", is a species of plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to Sri Lanka and parts of southern India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Leucas zeylanica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Leucas zeylanica is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of Ayurvedic and Siddha medicines, as well as in the preparation of local remedies for respiratory and gastrointestinal ailments.
